# GitHub Copilot Instructions

## Project Overview
This is a clone of the Humm Love website, a frontend project focused on replicating the design and functionality of the original Humm Love site.

## Repository Context
- **Purpose**: Educational clone project to practice web development skills
- **Tech Stack**: HTML, CSS, JavaScript (frontend technologies)
- **Focus**: UI/UX replication and responsive design

## Coding Standards

### General Guidelines
- Write clean, readable, and maintainable code
- Follow modern web development best practices
- Ensure cross-browser compatibility
- Prioritize responsive design for mobile, tablet, and desktop views

### HTML
- Use semantic HTML5 elements
- Maintain proper document structure and hierarchy
- Include appropriate meta tags and accessibility attributes
- Keep markup clean and well-indented

### CSS
- Use meaningful class names that describe purpose
- Follow a consistent naming convention (e.g., BEM or similar)
- Organize styles logically (layout, components, utilities)
- Prefer CSS Grid and Flexbox for layouts
- Write mobile-first responsive CSS
- Avoid inline styles

### JavaScript
- Use modern ES6+ syntax
- Write descriptive variable and function names
- Keep functions small and focused on a single responsibility
- Add comments for complex logic
- Handle errors appropriately
- Minimize DOM manipulation for better performance

### File Organization
- Keep related files together
- Use clear, descriptive file names
- Separate concerns (structure, style, behavior)

## Development Workflow
- Test changes across different browsers and devices
- Validate HTML and CSS for standards compliance
- Optimize images and assets for web performance
- Ensure accessibility standards are met (WCAG guidelines)

## Best Practices
- Always commit working code
- Write meaningful commit messages
- Keep commits focused and atomic
- Document significant decisions or complex implementations
